entities.name: Osiris

attributes.role: god of the dead, judge of the dead
attributes.mythical motif: shut up in an ark for one year

entities.raw_quote: not only were the bull and lingam his symbols, but he is also identified with Iswara or Osiris by the titles 'Iswara' and 'Mahe shwara,' 'The Great Iswara.'

entities.name: Siva

attributes.role: Destroyer, later identified with Brahma and Vishnu as part of Trimurti; Phallic god
attributes.mythical motif: makes a voyage during the Deluge on the ship Argha

entities.raw_quote: Siva is represented as making a voyage during the Deluge on the ship Argha?

entities.name: Nimrod

attributes.role: founder of first great empire, first idolater
attributes.mythical motif: deified after death; established Sun and Phallic worship

entities.raw_quote: But the worship of ancestors seems to have been a part of the idolatry propagated by Nimrod...

entities.name: Cushite race

attributes.role: pre-Aryan inhabitants of India, Cushitic/Ethiopian origin

entities.raw_quote: Professor Kawlinson says that 'linguistic research shows that a Cushite or Ethiopian race extended along the shores of the Southern Ocean from Abyssinia to India'

entities.name: Rama

attributes.role: Sun god, chief of Surya race

entities.raw_quote: Colonel Tod...observes, 'From Rama all the tribes named the Surya Vausa, or race of the Sun, claim descent.'

entities.name: Cama (Kham/Ham)

attributes.role: god of desire; identified with Osiris and Horus

entities.raw_quote: Cama was originally Kham or Ham, but, as in other cases, was ultimately identified with his grandson Nimrod.

entities.name: Yama

attributes.role: judge of the dead, king of Hades

entities.raw_quote: The Indian 'Yama' seems to be another form of Osiris. Like the latter, he is the judge of the dead...

entities.name: Gama

attributes.role: god of desire; son of Luksmi (Venus)

entities.raw_quote: 'Gama deva' is a youth like Cupid...with his arrows creates desire...

entities.name: Suttees

attributes.role: widows who self-immolate on husband’s funeral pyre

entities.raw_quote: The Suttees, who devoted themselves on the funeral pyres of their husbands, were considered to become pure by burning

entities.name: Buddha (Sakya Muni)

attributes.role: enlightened one; mortal avatar of supreme Buddha

entities.raw_quote: Sakya Muni...is represented to have become 'Buddha,' or 'enlightened,' i.e., he had attained to the wisdom of God...

summary: This chunk traces comparative mythology across Egypt, India, and the Near East, arguing for a Cushite origin of phallic/Sun worship and linking Osiris, Siva, Nimrod, and other deities as overlapping figures. It emphasizes ancestor veneration, funerary rites (e.g., suttee), and the identification of Indian Yama with Osiris, as well as early Buddhist cosmology involving multiple Buddhas.
key_passages: But the worship of ancestors seems to have been a part of the idolatry propagated by Nimrod, for we are told that Osiris built a temple in Egypt to his grandfather Ham...
Siva...is represented as making a voyage during the Deluge on the ship Argha?
The Indian 'Yama' seems to be another form of Osiris. Like the latter, he is the judge of the dead, and weighs their good actions against their bad actions...
Cama was originally Kham or Ham...and his wife was 'Rhytia,' who are manifestly the same as Cama and Reti.
'The Suttees, who devoted themselves on the funeral pyres of their husbands, were considered to become pure by burning'
